title: base_url path check relies on undocumented url::Url path() invariant
description: The check 'if path \!= "/"' works because url::Url (WHATWG URL standard) always returns "/" as the path for authority-based URLs with no path component. This is correct but non-obvious: a contributor reading 'path \!= "/"' would expect it rejects the root path, but it actually accepts it. The comment '// Store without trailing slash' on the line after the check is about the stored form, not about the path validation logic. Add a comment above the check: // url::Url returns "/" for authority-based URLs with no path component (WHATWG URL spec §6.1); this rejects any non-root path like "/api" or "/jmap"
